title: StudioDash Automation & Sync
seoTitle: StudioDash Automation and Sync | BarkinMad Studios
description: StudioDash sync behavior, provider health, manual refresh flows, and roadmap automation areas.
summary: StudioDash sync and automation features are designed to keep provider data understandable, traceable, and clearly separated from roadmap automation.

sections.heading: Sync principles
paragraphs: StudioDash is designed to make provider data state visible. A useful dashboard should show when data was last checked, whether a provider needs attention, and whether data is unavailable because a provider is unconfigured or has not returned data yet.
Provider-dependent cards remain visible so users can understand which integration is needed instead of wondering why a section disappeared.

sections.heading: Provider health
paragraphs: Provider health is a core part of the StudioDash experience. The app uses clear status language so users can distinguish healthy providers from providers needing attention, errors, not configured providers, and planned integrations.
bullets: Healthy: provider access and recent data state look usable.
Attention Required: configuration or provider data needs review.
Error: provider access, permissions, or data fetch failed.
Not Configured: the provider has not been set up for live data.
Planned: the integration is represented as roadmap or placeholder coverage.

sections.heading: Automation roadmap
paragraphs: Automation features such as scheduled reports, richer refresh scheduling, forecasting, and AI-assisted summaries should be described as roadmap content unless publicly released.
The public product promise is that StudioDash aims to reduce dashboard switching and make repeated checks easier, not that every future automation is already available.
